sutta: the buddha said there are monks there are these 5 powers what 5 the power of faith the power of diligence the power of mindfulness or recollection the power of concentration and the power of wisdom these are the 5 powers what is the power of energy sorry what is the power of faith the power of faith is is just as before what we read before that the aryan disciple has faith and believes in the enlightenment of the tata gather etc and what is the power of energy then also we heard before the power of energy is the aryan disciple abiding in active energy puts away all unskillful states and takes the skillful states she shows not the burden of skillful states this is the power of diligence what is the power mindfulness here in monks the aryan disciple is mindful or collected he is endowed with the highest mindfulness and discrimination he remembers and calls to mind what was said and done long ago this monks is called the power of mindfulness and what is the power of concentration here in monks the aryan disciple secluded from sense desires secluded from unwholesome mental states enters upon and abides in the first jhana which is accompanied by applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of seclusion with distilling of applied and sustained thought he enters upon and abides in the second jana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of concentration and with the fading away of delight he abides in equanimity and collected and mindful feeling pleasure with the body he enters upon and abides in the third jannah on account of which the noble ones say he has a joyful abiding who has equanimity and is collected and with the abandoning of bodily pleasure and the previous abandoning of bodily pain by the fading away of grief and joy he enters upon and abides in the fourth jhana which has neither pain nor pleasure and has complete purity of recollection or mindfulness and equanimity this monks is called the power of concentration and what is the power of wisdom is as before the aaron disciple has wisdom he is endowed with wisdom into the nature of growth and decay with iron penetration of the way to the utter destruction of dukkha this is the power of wisdom these monks are the 5 powers that's the end of the suta
commentary: here we come to the standard 5 powers the earlier 1 was the learner's powers this is the standard 5 things which the buddha calls the powers and the first 1 faith we have already dealt with second 1 is energy we've also dealt with third 1 is mindfulness the word is sati this word sati comes from a word no which is spelled s m r t i it's merti or something and it means to remember so basically sati means to remember you know uh and generally people like to use the word mindfulness but if you look at this definition of the word sati it says the aaron disciple is mindful he is endowed with the highest mindfulness and discrimination he remembers and calls to mind what was said and done long ago he remembers and calls to mind what was said and done long ago so this is the ability to remember so when we use the word mindfulness you must know mindfulness has 2 meanings 1 is to be generally a mindfulness aware of what is happening around you that is not the real meaning of the word sati the second meaning of mindfulness is the ability to recollect to remember and that is the more correct definition according to the words here he remembers and calls to mind what was said and done long ago so this we have to remember because in the word sati and the sutas the buddha says that sati is concerned with remembering the nature of our body and our mind the 5 aggregates body feeling perception volition and consciousness because that is where our problem lies that is where our ego is tied up to we always associate the self with these 5 things so we must always to remember to to look and see that these 5 things are not the cell then the power of concentration is the standard definition of how the 4 janas are attained you you find the buddha's description of samadhi is always connected with these 4 jaanas or sometimes it's just 1 pointedness of mind and this definition as explained here is the proper definition of concentration nowadays we have to be very careful because certain teachers their definition of concentration they use such words like vipassana jana when you look into the the definition of a personal genre it means the mindfulness from object to object and mindfulness from object to object is just sati it's just it's just a mindfulness uh i just mindfulness or recollection and it is the seventh factor of the aryan eightfold path whereas the the 4 janas are the eighth factor and these are 2 separate factors you must not confuse and mix them up i think they are 1 factor it's very important to to realize this any definition of samadhi must be based on these 4 definitions of the 4 janas how you enter the forjanas and what qualities the jana has so if anybody teaches that such and such is jhana you have always to compare with this definition here in the powers of the 4 jaanas of obtaining the 4 janas suta number
